title: Codex lane structured report
description: Every Codex lane returns this via `codex exec --output-schema`. Fable reads ONLY this object and rules PASS/BLOCKED; it never re-parses code or re-runs tests to verify a lane. NOTE: OpenAI strict structured output requires every property to be listed in `required`; optional fields are expressed as nullable (type [X, null]) instead of being omitted from `required`.

objective_result.description: PASS = every acceptance target met and full suite green. BLOCKED = hit a genuine blocker (report it, do not paper over). PARTIAL = some targets met; explain in honest_issues.

acceptance.description: One row per acceptance target from the spec. Empty only if the spec defined no numeric targets.

baseline.description: value before the change, or null if unknown/not applicable

changes.description: One line per change, file:line + what. Empty array for read-only lanes.

full_suite.description: Result of running the FULL blast-radius suite named in the spec (not a subset).

command.description: exact pytest command run (or the read-only git-status check the spec authorizes)

failures_all_preexisting.description: true ONLY if every failure is proven to also fail at HEAD before this change. If false and failed>0, objective_result cannot be PASS.

failure_notes.description: for each failure: name + why it is pre-existing/unrelated, or why unresolved; null if failed=0

honest_issues.description: Unsoftened. Everything still wrong, every target missed, every thing you could not fix. Do not omit to look clean.

next.description: What you would do next, for Fable to weigh. A suggestion, not an auto-executed plan.

session_id.description: This lane's Codex session id if known, so Fable can `codex exec resume <id>` to iterate without re-specifying. null if unknown (Fable reads it from the log header).
